title: lonicera caucasica subsp. caucasica
intro: Lonicera caucasica subsp. caucasica is a species of flowering plant in the honeysuckle family, known for its fragrant white or pink flowers and edible red berries. The plant is believed to have medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Lonicera caucasica subsp. caucasica include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its berries can be eaten fresh or used to make jams and preserves.
